title: Declare enums with the default Int32 underlying type
messageFormat: Use the default Int32 underlying type for this enum unless a specific storage size is required.
summary: Unless an enum is constrained by interop, persistence, or a wire format, it should keep the default int underlying type. Smaller or larger integer types save little in practice, surprise readers, and can break code that assumes the conventional 32-bit storage.
If a non-default size is genuinely required (for example to match a native struct), keep the explicit type and document why.
